notes: Performance is excellent. Max settings minus anti-aliasing at 1440p, never dips below 60FPS once shaders are compiled. There are some minor graphical issues, specifically blood stains can become discoloured and sometimes there's minor flickering with hair. There is also an issue with mouse input where you eventually cannot pan any further left, I work around this by panning all the way to the right every so often. If the mouse issue can be fixed then the game should be perfectly playable.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: Completed the game with no issues. constant 75FPS (Vsync) 1080p Max settings (but 4x AA)

notes: A few visual glitches, audio in cutscenes was not working and mouse is sometimes glitchy when aiming left. Otherwise, runs very smoothly with highest graphics settings.
title: Max Payne 3

notes:
I rated a Platinum, however I experienced some textures missing once, a weird display of a hair texture once. Social Club Signs in. Single Player campaign works. Multiplayer works. The mouse aim and controls are smooth, I may have experience sthe mouse movement to right stopping short of the full way but can't say for certain as it did not impede gamplay in my session.

I finished the second and third part without any crashes or any real issues. I tested multiplayer it works.

As a note, this game now runs better under proton than it did on my same machine under Windows 10.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: Game is fully playable from start to finish but has several (very) minor graphical issues in some situations. The cursor behaves quite strangely and to get aiming working fully, I had to use winecfg to enable a virtual desktop at my native resolution, and automatically capture mouse in fullscreen windows. I also had to disable two of my three monitors. Using Lutris can automate this. After this, the game worked perfectly.
title: Max Payne 3

notes:
This game works perfectly well.
I finished the game in 10 - 12hrs and I didn't see any issue.
Depending on the level (a location), I had about 40 - 80FPS during the game.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: The game works flawlessly. The gameplay is smooth and I had no issues. I didn't have to do anything just hit install and play.
title: Max Payne 3

notes:
Runs smoothly except when lowering resolution in the game. As soon as I run the game in a resolution that is lesser than my desktop resolution I have issues with aiming right.

It's as if the cursor moves "outside of the window" and looses focus. It does not however happen when I move to the left. I notice that this also happens when in the menus. When I move the cursor all the way to the right the screen flickers a little drops in brightness (which is a desktop setting I have when windows lose focus).

When I set the resolution to 4K (which is my desktop resolution) everything works fine.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: Some distracting visual glitching on Neves' hair. No other unexpected visual anomalies/glitching other than that. Game runs smoothly once installed. You must also completely install the other softwares included (Rockstar Game Club) for the game to work.

notes: Works great with the recommended graphic settings.

notes: Ran perfect without any tweaks with zero loss in graphics quality.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: The Rockstar Social Club signup was a complete pain. The mouse cursor would disappear occasionally and there is no keyboard navigation support so this meant killing and restart the game. This is made worse by the pedantry of the username choosing process resulting in me having to go round and round trying to find a suitable user name. Once I got past that, I tried to configure the game and before I could reset the screen resolution to monitor native it was running in 800x600 and was placing a mouse gate blocking the cursor from the right quarter of the screen, preventing me from accepting the screen res change. I restarted and the mouse gate was slightly further right, allowing me to fix the issue. I did notice an oddity in that the key assignment screen would not accept the Enter key as a bound key. I figured this was just because the game reserved it for its own use. Unfortunately this turned out not to be the case. The enter key just doesn't work. This completely wrecks the game, and is the reason for the Borked rating. If you die in game, you end up in an inescapable loop: Retry (Enter) or Return to Menu (Esc). Enter doesn't work and Esc pops up this: Are you sure? Yes (Enter) No (Esc). Again, Enter is broken and Esc takes you back to the previous choice. Kill game time. It's a shame because other than that, I found no issues with the way the game ran on highest graphic settings.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: Runs out of the box. Rockstar Social Club crap was 100% done by steam itself - very nice! No issues after 2h of gaming.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: After ~7h still running fine. But from time to time sometimes audio in video scenes disappeared. I found out this was an issue with my alsa configuration. I'm not using a pulse audio sound system, I'm just using alsa apulse plugin, because I don't like pulse audio. If your sound card is lacking hardware mixing, make sure you properly configured dmix plugin for alsa, then everything works fine.
title: Max Payne 3

notes: The rockstar social club signup/login is problematic. In fullscreen mode the mouse pointer is not visible at all, so logging in and completing the captcha is impossible. If you set launch option "-windowed" you see the cursor until you click anything. Then you have to switch back and forth to desktop to see the cursor again. It's possible to get into the gameplay, but it's not easy as it used to be.
title: Max Payne 3
